_note: Pregnancy categories from official US FDA labeling (DailyMed/openFDA) and European SmPC where available. Note: FDA replaced A/B/C/D/X categories with narrative PLLR labels in 2015 for new approvals; older medicines retain letter categories. Indian CDSCO follows broadly similar classification. Always verify against current Indian SmPC.

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: CONTRAINDICATED
pregnancy_rules.reason: Statins may cause fetal harm. Cholesterol biosynthesis is critical for fetal development. Category X: risks outweigh any possible benefit.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: All trimesters

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: CONTRAINDICATED (2nd and 3rd trimester)
pregnancy_rules.reason: ARBs acting on the renin-angiotensin system in 2nd and 3rd trimester can cause fetal renal dysfunction, oligohydramnios, neonatal renal failure, skull hypoplasia, and death.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: Discontinue as soon as pregnancy detected

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: USE WITH CAUTION — consult physician
pregnancy_rules.reason: Animal studies show no risk, but adequate human studies are lacking. Some guidelines (UK NICE) use metformin in gestational diabetes under supervision. Not first-line in India without physician oversight.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: Requires specialist supervision if used

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: GENERALLY SAFE — first-line analgesic/antipyretic in pregnancy
pregnancy_rules.reason: Extensive human use data; no consistent evidence of teratogenicity. Preferred over NSAIDs and aspirin in pregnancy.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: Use minimum effective dose for shortest duration

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: AVOID — especially in 3rd trimester
pregnancy_rules.reason: High-dose aspirin in 3rd trimester associated with premature closure of ductus arteriosus, neonatal bleeding, and prolonged labor.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: Low-dose (75-100mg) may be used under physician supervision for pre-eclampsia prevention

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: AVOID unless no safer alternative
pregnancy_rules.reason: Animal studies show arthropathy in immature animals. Human data insufficient. Potential risk to developing cartilage.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: Avoid throughout pregnancy if alternatives exist

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: SAFE — essential to maintain in pregnancy
pregnancy_rules.reason: Hypothyroidism untreated in pregnancy causes fetal neurological harm. Levothyroxine is physiological replacement and is safe; dose often needs increase during pregnancy.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: Monitor TSH every trimester; dose adjustments required

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: GENERALLY CONSIDERED SAFE — use with caution
pregnancy_rules.reason: Animal studies show no fetal harm. Limited human data but widely used in pregnancy for certain indications.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: Use only when clearly needed

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: USE WITH CAUTION — limited human data
pregnancy_rules.reason: Animal studies show no fetal harm; human data limited. Use only if clearly needed.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: Generally discontinued before planned delivery

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: USE WITH CAUTION — epilepsy treatment must continue
pregnancy_rules.reason: Seizures during pregnancy pose serious maternal and fetal risks. Levetiracetam may be continued under neurologist supervision. Enroll in pregnancy registry.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: Folic acid supplementation recommended. Plasma levels may decrease in pregnancy; monitor.

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: ABSOLUTELY CONTRAINDICATED — highest teratogenicity
pregnancy_rules.reason: Neural tube defects (1-2%), cardiac defects, cleft palate, neurodevelopmental disorders including autism and reduced IQ. Must not be used in women of childbearing potential without specialist review and Pregnancy Prevention Programme compliance.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: All trimesters. Patients must be counseled and enrolled in prevention program.
pregnancy_rules.source_name: FDA Black Box Warning / EMA SmPC

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: ABSOLUTELY CONTRAINDICATED
pregnancy_rules.reason: Methotrexate is a folate antagonist and potent teratogen. Associated with miscarriage, neural tube defects, skeletal abnormalities. FDA Pregnancy Category X.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: All trimesters. Contraception mandatory during use and for 3 months after stopping in both sexes.

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: HIGH RISK — avoid if alternatives exist
pregnancy_rules.reason: Carbamazepine causes neural tube defects (spina bifida), craniofacial defects, growth retardation. Risk is 2-3x baseline. Folic acid 5mg/day mandatory if used in pregnancy.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: All trimesters. Use only if seizure control otherwise impossible.

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: CONTRAINDICATED especially 1st trimester and last weeks before delivery
pregnancy_rules.reason: 1st trimester: warfarin embryopathy (nasal hypoplasia, stippled epiphyses, limb hypoplasia). Any trimester: fetal/neonatal bleeding. Near-term: neonatal haemorrhage.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: Switch to LMWH in 1st trimester and near delivery. May be acceptable mid-pregnancy for mechanical valves under specialist care.

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: AVOID — use only if no alternative
pregnancy_rules.reason: Neonatal thyroid dysfunction (hypothyroidism and hyperthyroidism) due to iodine content. Bradycardia, QT prolongation in fetus. Long half-life means effects can persist.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: All trimesters. Neonatal thyroid screening mandatory if exposed in pregnancy.

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: CONTRAINDICATED in 2nd and 3rd trimester
pregnancy_rules.reason: ACE inhibitors in 2nd/3rd trimester cause fetal renal tubular dysplasia, oligohydramnios, neonatal anuric renal failure, skull hypoplasia.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: Discontinue immediately when pregnancy diagnosed. Switch to methyldopa or labetalol for hypertension in pregnancy.

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: AVOID — especially from 20 weeks onwards
pregnancy_rules.reason: NSAIDs from 20 weeks: fetal renal dysfunction causing oligohydramnios and potential limb contractures. From 30 weeks: premature closure of ductus arteriosus (pulmonary hypertension). From 32 weeks: CONTRAINDICATED.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: Avoid from 20 weeks if possible. Absolutely avoid from 30 weeks. Paracetamol is the safe alternative.

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: CONTRAINDICATED
pregnancy_rules.reason: Tetracyclines deposit in developing teeth and bone: permanent yellow-brown discolouration of teeth, enamel hypoplasia, inhibition of bone growth.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: All trimesters. Safe alternatives include amoxicillin, azithromycin (with caution).

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: SINGLE LOW DOSE (150mg): probably low risk; HIGH DOSE / PROLONGED: CONTRAINDICATED
pregnancy_rules.reason: Multiple-dose or high-dose fluconazole associated with rare fetal skeletal and cardiac malformations. Single 150mg dose for vaginal candidiasis generally considered low risk but to be avoided if possible.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: Use only if topical treatment fails. Single dose only in 1st trimester if absolutely necessary.

pregnancy_rules.risk_level: CONTRAINDICATED in 1st trimester; avoid in pregnancy
pregnancy_rules.reason: Animal studies show embryotoxicity and teratogenicity. WHO recommends avoiding in first trimester; use only after risk-benefit assessment in 2nd/3rd trimester for systemic parasitic infections.
pregnancy_rules.trimester_specifics: Avoid 1st trimester. In later pregnancy for hydatid disease, may be necessary under specialist supervision.
